body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:interrupt

## Scenario

user-esc-interrupt: the user cancels a long in-flight turn with the interrupt key before the agent finishes; state resolves cleanly to `ready`, and the next prompt proves the parser reset. User-observable signal (scenario-meanings): `ready → working → ready`; state does not stick in `working` post-interrupt, then a clean recovery arc.

## Agent supports it

opencode's interactive TUI exposes a mid-turn cancel (Escape / Ctrl-C aborts the in-flight turn), and on an aborted turn the model run reports a finish reason of `interrupted`. So the agent CAN be driven into the ready→working→[interrupt]→ready arc the scenario exercises.

## daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`): the daemon reads session state straight from the SQLite store. The parser explicitly handles the interrupt finish reason — `parser.go:158` `case "interrupted"` maps a `step-finish` part with reason=interrupted to `turn_done` (the comment at parser.go:90 reads `reason="interrupted" → user cancelled (Ctrl+C) → emit "turn_done"`), and the watcher's `isTerminalPart` (`watcher.go:435`) accepts `interrupted` among its terminal reasons {stop, interrupted, length, error, content-filter}. So once a real interrupted-turn step-finish part lands in the store, the daemon settles the session working→ready cleanly. The daemon is NOT the blocker — this is a genuine `daemon_capability: full`, distinct from the sibling `turn-aborted-by-error` cell where opencode never persists a step-finish part at all (errors land on `message.data.error`, not as a part). An ESC-cancelled turn DOES finish as a `step-finish` reason=interrupted part, which the parser branch above consumes.

## Why this cell is NOT recordable today (driver gap: interrupt)

The scenario requires interrupting an IN-FLIGHT turn. The opencode interactive driver (`.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh`) drives opencode through BLOCKING headless `opencode run` subprocesses and implements ONLY three step types in its `case "$TYPE"` dispatch: `send` (launches an `opencode run` subprocess and WAITS for it to complete), `wait_turn` (a no-op — `opencode run` already blocks until the turn ends), and `sleep`. There is NO `keys` step and NO `interrupt` step, and structurally there is no way to deliver a mid-turn ESC: each `send` blocks inside the subprocess until the turn is already over, so by the time the driver regains control there is no in-flight turn left to cancel. Eliciting a `step-finish` reason=interrupted part requires holding a live interactive opencode TUI open and sending Escape WHILE the turn streams — a primitive this send-only blocking-subprocess driver cannot express.

### Not a false gap (and not caught by recipe-lint)

The claudecode / aider / pi rows for this scenario all use an `interrupt` step in their recipes (driven by tmux send-keys into a long-lived REPL). The opencode driver has no REPL to send keys into — it shells out to `opencode run` per turn. Unlike a slash command that takes an inline argument (sendable via the `send` path), a mid-turn ESC is fundamentally a live-keystroke primitive against a running turn; there is no send-only equivalent. recipe-lint.sh does NOT flag this: the recipe is left `applicable:false` with no `script`, so there is no step-TYPE for the lint to reject — the gap is the absence of a live-REPL interrupt CAPABILITY, mirroring the opencode model-switch-midsession driver_gap (gap:model-select), which recipe-lint likewise could not catch.

## What would unblock it

Replace or augment `drive-opencode-interactive.sh` with a LIVE-REPL driver (analogous to the tmux-based claudecode/pi/aider drivers): launch the opencode TUI in a tmux pane, `send` a long prompt, then fire a `keys`/`interrupt` step that delivers Escape mid-stream so opencode writes a `step-finish` reason=interrupted part. Once the driver can interrupt an in-flight turn, the cell becomes recordable: the daemon already parses reason=interrupted → turn_done from the store, so the working→ready settle and the recovery arc in expected.jsonl would validate.

## Matrix drift

The coverage matrix currently records opencode/user-esc-interrupt as `driver_capability: ready` (notes: "step-finish reason=interrupted handled"). That over-states the driver: the PARSER handles reason=interrupted (daemon_capability:full is correct), but the DRIVER cannot elicit it. The coverage cell should be updated to `driver_capability: gap:interrupt`.

## Confidence

0.85: the driver grammar (`send|wait_turn|sleep`, blocking `opencode run` per send, no keys/interrupt step) is read directly from the live driver source, and the parser's reason=interrupted → turn_done handling is read directly from parser.go / watcher.go. Not higher only because the unblocked recording itself (a real interrupted-turn step-finish part flowing through to a working→ready settle) has not been captured — that is the developer task this gap defers.
caveats: Driver-gap, not an agent or daemon limitation: opencode the agent supports mid-turn cancel and the daemon parses step-finish reason=interrupted → turn_done (parser.go:158, watcher.go:435). The blocker is purely the driver's missing live-REPL mid-turn interrupt primitive (gap:interrupt).

recipe-lint.sh does NOT catch this gap: the recipe is left applicable:false with no script, so there is no step-TYPE to reject; the missing capability is a live-REPL Escape-mid-turn primitive against a blocking `opencode run` subprocess (same shape as the opencode model-switch-midsession gap:model-select).

Distinct from the sibling turn-aborted-by-error cell (assessed daemon_capability:incapable): there opencode never persists a step-finish part for an error abort (errors land on message.data.error). An ESC-cancelled turn DOES finish as a step-finish reason=interrupted part, which the parser consumes — hence daemon_capability:full here.

sources.note: case "$TYPE" handles only send|wait_turn|sleep; each `send` launches a BLOCKING `opencode run` subprocess and waits for it to complete — no keys/interrupt step, and no way to deliver a mid-turn ESC against a turn that is already over by the time control returns
line 158 `case "interrupted"` maps step-finish reason=interrupted → turn_done (comment line 90: reason=interrupted → user cancelled (Ctrl+C) → emit turn_done) — daemon_capability:full
isTerminalPart (line 435) accepts reason in {stop, interrupted, length, error, content-filter} — an interrupted step-finish part is a terminal turn signal
opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; daemon reads session/turn state from SQLite, so a real interrupted step-finish part WOULD be observed as a clean working→ready settle
claudecode/aider/pi user-esc-interrupt recipes use an `interrupt` step (tmux send-keys into a live REPL); opencode has no live REPL to drive — it shells out to `opencode run` per turn

coverage cell currently driver_capability:ready ("step-finish reason=interrupted handled") — that conflates the PARSER handling it (true) with the DRIVER eliciting it (false); should update to gap:interrupt
